pattern: Reservoir sampling walks the list exactly once, replacing the held answer with node k's value at probability 1/k, so a chain of unknown length yields a uniform pick without ever materializing it.
hints: Copying every value into an array first works but spends O(n) memory the problem is nudging you away from.
Walk the list while keeping a running count of nodes seen so far and a single tentative answer.
At the k-th node, replace the tentative answer with that node's value with probability 1/k — by induction every node ends up equally likely once the walk finishes.

edge_inputs.description: single-node list makes getRandom fully determined
short distinct-value list sampled many times
duplicate values in the list — legal answers collapse to a smaller set than the node count
every node carries the same value
two-node list with negative and positive extremes
longer list, repeated sampling to exercise the running-count reservoir logic

stress.description: random-length list of random values, repeated getRandom draws
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 50)))
    values = [rng.randint(-10000, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    ops = ['Solution']
    args = [[values]]
    calls = max(1, min(size, 60))
    for _ in range(calls):
        ops.append('getRandom')
        args.append([])
    return [ops, args]
